steps.title: Find the slope of s
steps.content:
Rewrite line $s$ in slope-intercept form:
$$
6x+2y=0
$$
$$
2y=-6x
$$
$$
y=-3x
$$
So line $s$ has slope $-3$.

steps.title: Use perpendicular slope
steps.content: A perpendicular line has slope equal to the negative reciprocal. The negative reciprocal of $-3$ is $\frac{1}{3}$, so line $t$ has slope $\frac{1}{3}$.

steps.title: Use the two points
steps.content: The slope between $(5,0)$ and $(k,\frac{4}{3})$ is $\frac{1}{3}$: $$ \frac{\frac{4}{3}-0}{k-5}=\frac{1}{3} $$ $$ 4=k-5 $$ $$ k=9 $$ The answer is 9.
